etymology.description: 並(並) (bìng, 'side by side') + 日(日) (rì, 'sun') = the sun shining everywhere -> universal
etymology.interpretation: The character 普(pǔ, 'universal') combines 並(並), meaning 'side by side', with 日, meaning 'sun', to convey the concept of the sun shining everywhere simultaneously, symbolizing universality.

examples.chinese: 普遍的问题让大家都很烦恼(普遍的問題讓大家都很煩惱)。
examples.pinyin: Pǔbiàn de wèntí ràng dàjiā dōu hěn fánnǎo.
examples.english: Universal problems make everyone feel troubled.

examples.chinese: 这个观点很普遍(這個觀點很普遍)，所以没有人反对(所以沒有人反對)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège guāndiǎn hěn pǔbiàn, suǒyǐ méiyǒu rén fǎnduì.
examples.english: This viewpoint is very universal, so nobody opposes it.

examples.chinese: 使用普适的解决方案(使用普適的解決方案)，能够帮助更多的人(能夠幫助更多的人)。
examples.pinyin: Shǐyòng pǔshì de jiějué fāng'àn, nénggòu bāngzhù gèng duō de rén.
examples.english: Using a universal solution can help more people.
